chief_complaint: 73-year-old woman with an acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) requiring emergency intubation and mechanical ventilation for acute respiratory failure.
history: The patient suffered from profound hypoxia and hypercapnia with uncompensated respiratory acidosis. She was administered an intravenous bolus of 500 mg of ketamine for prehospital anesthesia.
